title: The Complete Guide to Installing eSIM on iPhone and Android Devices
description: Bought an eSIM plan and wondering how to install it? Don't worry because it's much simpler than you think.
body:
Reading time: 7 minutes

Everything You Need to Know About Activating eSIM

Bought an eSIM plan and wondering how to install it? Don't worry because it's much simpler than you think. In this guide, we'll walk you through every step of the process to ensure a smooth installation experience.

Before You Start - Important Checks

First, you need to ensure your device supports eSIM technology. Compatible devices include iPhone XS and all later models, which covers the iPhone 11, 12, 13, 14, and 15 series. For Android users, Samsung Galaxy S20 and later models support eSIM, as do Google Pixel 3 and later versions. Most new Huawei and Xiaomi models also include eSIM capability, though you should verify your specific model.

Installing eSIM on iPhone Using QR Code

After purchasing a plan from Sparks eSIM, you'll receive an email containing a QR code for installation. To begin the installation, open your iPhone's Settings app and navigate to the Cellular or Cellular Data section. Look for the option that says Add Cellular Plan and tap on it. The camera will open automatically, allowing you to scan the QR code you received via email. Hold your phone steady over the code and wait a few seconds for the plan to load and install.

Once the installation completes, you'll have the option to give the new plan a name such as "Europe Trip" or "Travel Data" to help you distinguish it from your regular SIM. Finally, you need to choose which plan will serve as the default for cellular data and which plan will handle calls and SMS messages. Most travelers prefer to use the eSIM for data while keeping their home SIM for calls and messages.

Installing eSIM on Android Devices

For Samsung device owners, the process begins by opening Settings and navigating to Connections, then SIM Card Manager. From there, select Add Cellular Plan and you'll have the option to either scan the QR code or enter the details manually. After scanning, you can activate the eSIM immediately or wait until you arrive at your destination.

Google Pixel users should open Settings and go to Network & Internet, then tap the plus sign next to SIMs. Select the option to Download SIM instead of inserting a physical card. You can then scan the QR code or enter the activation details manually if scanning isn't possible.

Manual Installation Without QR Code

If you don't have access to a QR code scanner or prefer manual entry, Sparks provides manual activation details along with the QR code. Select the option to Enter Details Manually in your device settings. You'll need to enter three key pieces of information: the SM-DP+ Address, the Activation Code, and a Confirmation Code if required. Make sure to enter these exactly as provided to avoid installation errors.

Activating Your eSIM at the Destination

This step is very important: do not activate the eSIM before arriving at your destination. Activating early will start the validity period and waste precious days. Upon arrival at your destination, go to your device settings and activate the eSIM. Make sure that Data Roaming is enabled for the eSIM, as this is necessary for it to connect to local networks. Allow up to five minutes for the network to connect and establish a data connection. You can test the connection by opening a web browser or any app that requires internet access.

Solving Common Problems

If you see "No Service" or "No Network Found" messages, first check that Data Roaming is enabled for the eSIM. Try selecting a network manually in your cellular settings rather than relying on automatic selection. Sometimes a simple device restart can resolve connection issues.

If the eSIM isn't activated or working, ensure that the plan is still valid and hasn't expired. Check that there's enough data remaining in your allocation. If problems persist, contact Sparks support for assistance with technical issues.

If data seems to be depleting faster than expected, check which apps are using background data in your settings. Turn off automatic updates for apps and operating system features. Use WiFi whenever possible for large downloads or updates to preserve your cellular data.

Important Tips for Success

Install the eSIM a day before your flight while you're still at home in a relaxed environment. Take a screenshot of the QR code and save it in your photos for backup in case you need to reinstall. Write down the manual activation details in a note or on paper as a backup. Ensure your device is updated to the latest operating system version for the best compatibility and fewest bugs.

Frequently Asked Questions

Many travelers ask whether they can use eSIM and their regular SIM together, and the answer is yes. That's exactly the advantage of dual SIM functionality, allowing you to receive calls on your Israeli SIM while browsing with the eSIM. If you change devices during your trip or shortly after, Sparks eSIM is reusable so you can install it on a new device. You don't need to remove the eSIM after the trip ends, though you can delete it from settings if you want to clean up your device.
